BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X".       failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.

      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.

      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.

      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.

      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).

===SCRIPT===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Regenerate the hash-pinned lockfiles from pyproject.toml (#G-016).
#
# Dependabot bumps the version ranges in pyproject.toml but cannot regenerate the uv lockfiles,
# so a Dependabot PR will fail the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until the lockfiles are
# regenerated. Run this on a Dependabot branch (or any PR that changes pyproject dependencies),
# then commit the updated requirements*.lock files:
#
#     scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh && git commit -am "chore: regenerate lockfiles"
#
# uv is pinned to the same version CI uses (#F-031) so the output matches the sync check exactly.
# The resolution is ALSO pinned to a fixed point-in-time index via --exclude-newer (#K-OPS-1): the
# date lives in .uv-exclude-newer (single source of truth, read by CI too) so the regen is
# deterministic. Without it, uv resolves the NEWEST in-range version of every (largely
# unconstrained, transitive) dep at wall-clock time, so the committed lock would drift the instant
# any upstream publishes an in-range release and the "in sync" gate would go red on unrelated PRs.
# To intentionally pick up new releases, bump the date in .uv-exclude-newer and re-run this script.
#
# This regenerates ALL THREE gated lockfiles — requirements.lock, requirements-core.lock, AND
# requirements-build.lock (#K-OPS-2): the build lock is gated by CI too, so omitting it left a
# maintainer following this script still red on a build-backend bump with no scripted fix.
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

UV_VERSION="0.11.21"
EXCLUDE_NEWER="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .uv-exclude-newer)"

if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ "$(uv --version | awk '{print $2}')" != "$UV_VERSION" ]; then
  echo "installing uv==$UV_VERSION (CI-pinned) ..." >&2
  python -m pip install "uv==$UV_VERSION" >&2
fi

uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes \
  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements.lock
uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes \
  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements-core.lock
printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes \
  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements-build.lock

echo "regenerated requirements.lock + requirements-core.lock + requirements-build.lock (exclude-newer=$EXCLUDE_NEWER) — review and commit them."
===DIFF SCRIPT===

I have a noted concern: the test asserts mere substring presence of `requirements-build.lock`. There are now 3 mentions (1 comment, 1 actual `-o` command, 1 echo). If someone reverted only the executable `uv pip compile ... -o requirements-build.lock` line but kept the comment/echo, the test would still pass while the script no longer regenerates the build lock. The test is a config-presence guard, not behavioral.

However, against the *actual* historical bug (pre-fix script had zero mentions), the test does discriminate. And practically, the comment and echo were added in the same fix; the realistic revert (removing the fix) removes all three. The test is weaker than a behavioral one but does catch the regression class it targets. This is a minor robustness gap, not a residual functional gap — the fix itself is correct, complete, and matches CI verbatim (verified by reproducing the committed lock).

input.rationale: Root cause is correctly identified and fixed at the right layer. scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh previously regenerated only requirements.lock and requirements-core.lock (zero references to requirements-build.lock, confirmed via git show d08f031~1), while CI's audit job gates all three (ci.yml lines 154-157 compile and diff requirements-build.lock, failing red on staleness). A maintainer following the documented script on a build-backend bump stayed red with no scripted fix. The fix adds the build-lock compile step using the IDENTICAL invocation CI runs: `printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in; uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements-build.lock`. I reproduced this command (exclude-newer=2026-06-14T00:00:00Z from .uv-exclude-newer) with uv 0.11.21 and the regenerated build lock diffs CLEAN against the committed requirements-build.lock — so script output == CI's regen == committed file. CI error messages for all three locks point at scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh (lines 146/151/157), and dependabot.yml references the script. The test test_regenerate_script_covers_all_gated_lockfiles passes; against the pre-fix script (no build-lock reference) its loop assertion would have failed, so it discriminates against the actual historical regression. Full 40-test supply-chain suite passes.
concerns: Test strength: test_regenerate_script_covers_all_gated_lockfiles only asserts substring presence of 'requirements-build.lock' in the script text. The fix introduced THREE mentions (comment line 20, the load-bearing `-o` compile command line 40, and the echo line 42). A partial revert that deleted only the executable `uv pip compile ... -o requirements-build.lock` line while leaving the comment or echo would leave the test green yet the script non-functional. It is a config-presence guard, not behavioral (it does not run the script and diff the output against the committed lock, the way the CI gate does). This discriminates against the exact historical bug (which had zero mentions) but not against every revert of the functional line. Minor robustness gap, not a residual functional gap.
